macro_segments.title: Introduction to Moredet: A Wife Who Rebels Against Her Husband

micro_segments.title: Transition from Onah (Marital Relations) to Moredet (Rebellion)
The Basic Mishnah: Monetary Penalties for a Moredet
The Reciprocal Case: A Mored (Husband) Who Fails His Obligations

macro_segments.title: Dispute Over What Constitutes Moredet: Sex Versus Domestic Labor

micro_segments.title: Rav Huna's Position: Moredet Means Refusing Sexual Relations
Rabbi Yose bar Chanina's Position: Moredet Means Refusing Domestic Work
Tosafot's Analysis: The Debate Reflects Woman's Right to Refuse Work in Exchange for Sustenance

macro_segments.title: Application to the Reciprocal Case: What Does a Mored (Refusing Husband) Parallel?

micro_segments.title: If Moredet Means Sex Refusal, Mored Means Sex Refusal by Husband
If Moredet Means Labor Refusal, Mored Means Refusing to Provide Food/Support
Rav's Teaching: A Husband Refusing Support Must Immediately Divorce and Pay Ketubah
Gemara's Resolution: Even with Immediate Divorce Obligation, We Allow Time for Change Through Increasing Ketubah Amount

macro_segments.title: The Rabbis' Later Enactment: Replacing Progressive Fines with a Four-Week Announcement Process

micro_segments.title: The Enactment: Four Public Announcements in Synagogues, Then Immediate Loss of Ketubah
Dispute on Engagement: Two Warnings or One Warning (Beginning vs. End of Process)?
Rava vs. Rav Huna bar Yehuda: Do We Actually Negotiate with the Woman During the Four Weeks?

macro_segments.title: The Case of 'Ma'is Alai': When a Woman Finds Her Husband Repulsive and Refuses Sex

micro_segments.title: Ameimark's Distinction: Woman Refusing as Leverage vs. Woman Saying Marriage is Intolerable
Rav Sheshet vs. Mar Zutra: Do We Force Compliance Through Financial Penalties Even When She Says Marriage is Unbearable?
Mar Zutra's Case Study: Woman Forced to Stay, Produces Great Offspring, but Gemara Rejects This as Proof
Broader Implication: Can We Compel Divorce if Woman Demands It (Ma'is Alai), and the Agunah Problem

macro_segments.title: Details of Ketubah Loss and the Case of Rav Zevid's Daughter-in-Law

micro_segments.title: What Does a Moredet Lose? Including Her Own Clothes or Only Worn Garments?
Alternative Rulings: One-Year Process Without Food Support vs. Immediate Divorce
Post-Talmudic Practice (Tosafot Citing Rav Sherira and Savorai Rabbanan): Immediate Divorce and Return of Clothes

macro_segments.title: Moredet Among Arusah and Shomeret Yavam: When Can We Apply These Rules?

micro_segments.title: Rav Tuvi and Shmuel: Writing a Writ of Rebellion Against an Arusah But Not a Shomeret Yavam
Braitah Objection: Earlier Source Says Same Rules Apply to Both Arusah and Shomeret Yavam
Resolution: Different Cases—Who Is Making the Demand (Him vs. Her) Determines Whether We Intervene

macro_segments.title: Yibum vs. Chalitzah: Preferring Chalitzah and Refusing to Compel Yibum

micro_segments.title: Why Not Compel a Woman to Accept Yibum? She Can Remain a Shomeret Yavam or Demand Chalitzah
Why Compel Chalitzah? Woman's Legitimate Claim: She Wants Children for Her Old Age
Historical Shift: From Preferring Yibum to Preferring Chalitzah Due to Loss of Sincere Motivation
Parallel to Earlier Questions: What Measures Do We Use to Force Compliance on Each Side?

topical_tags.term: Moredet (woman rebelling/refusing duties)
Mored (man rebelling/refusing duties)
Ketubah (marriage contract; loss of)
Sexual relations (tashmish hamitah) vs. domestic labor (melacha)
Ma'is alai (husband/marriage is repulsive/intolerable)
Agunah (chained wife, divorce problem)
Shalom Bayis (peace in the home; marital harmony)
Get (divorce; compelling husband to divorce)
Mezonot (sustenance; food support obligation)
Rav Huna (holds moredet = sex refusal)
Rabbi Yose bar Chanina (holds moredet = labor refusal)
Tosafot (medieval commentary; analyzes disputes)
Rambam (Maimonides; rules woman can demand divorce via ma'is alai)
Rav Sherira Gaon (post-Talmudic authority; immediate divorce)
Rabbanan Savorai (trans-Talmudic rabbis; immediate divorce enactment)
Rava (holds we engage woman during process)
Rav Huna bar Yehuda (holds we do not engage woman, only announce)
Mar Zutra (forces compliance even in ma'is alai case)
Arusah (betrothed woman, not yet married)
Shomeret Yavam (woman awaiting yibum/levirate marriage)
Yibum (levirate marriage obligation)
Chalitzah (removal of shoe, alternative to yibum)
Niddah (menstruant; ritual impurity affecting sexual relations)
Public announcement (machrizin) in synagogues
Monetary fine (reducing ketubah weekly or via announcement)
Dinar and Tarpeikin (currency denominations)
Rav's teaching on immediate obligation
Beit Din (rabbinic court; role and intervention)
Historical context: women converting to Islam to escape marriage
Rav Tuvi bar Kisna and Shmuel (on arusah vs. shomeret yavam)
Rav Pada and Rabbi Yochanan (preferring chalitzah)
Kashe (difficulty; objection in Gemara)
